---
title: "Medical Data Science Curriculum"
subtitle: "Introductory Course — Division of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence, FK Udayana"
about:
  template: marquee
  links:
    - text: Start Introductory Course
      href: intro/week-01/index.qmd
    - text: Curriculum Overview
      href: overview.qmd
    - text: Basic Tier
      href: basic/index.qmd
    - text: Intermediate Tier
      href: intermediate/index.qmd
    - text: Advanced Tier
      href: advanced/index.qmd
    - text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/balinesthesia/mds-curriculum
      icon: github
---

## Introductory Course

**Duration:** 4 weeks  
**Goal:** Zero-assumption entry path — establish shared toolchain competence before Basic tier content begins

Residents arrive with clinical intelligence but no programming or statistics background. The Introductory Course separates tool learning from concept learning. Without this foundation, Week 1 of Basic would require simultaneous learning of tools and concepts — a proven failure mode.

---

## Learning Goals

By the end of this course, residents will:

1. Navigate the command line confidently (terminal, file system, paths)
2. Use Git and GitHub for version control (clone, commit, push, fork)
3. Render a Quarto document with R code chunks
4. Understand core programming concepts (variables, data types, functions)
5. Refresh basic statistics intuition (mean, median, variance, distributions, p-values)

---

## Week Overview

| Week | Topic | Key Skills |
|------|-------|------------|
| [1](intro/week-01/index.qmd) | Command line basics | Terminal navigation, file operations |
| [2](intro/week-02/index.qmd) | Git and GitHub | Version control, repository workflow |
| [3](intro/week-03/index.qmd) | R + Quarto orientation | Literate programming, document rendering |
| [4](intro/week-04/index.qmd) | Programming + Statistics | Variables, functions, summary statistics |

---

## Prerequisites

- Clinical intelligence (you have this)
- No programming or statistics background assumed
- Personal laptop (Windows, macOS, or Linux)

---

## Full Curriculum

The Introductory Course is the entry point to a four-tier curriculum. See the [Curriculum Overview](overview.qmd) for the complete structure: Basic → Intermediate → Advanced.
